title: rosaceae
intro: The rosaceae rank, also known as the rose family, is a group of flowering plants that are primarily shrubs or small trees with simple, often pinnate leaves and showy flowers. This family is characterized by the presence of five-petaled flowers and the formation of aggregate fruits, such as pome or drupe, which are often fleshy and edible. Rosaceae includes a wide range of species, from wild roses and apple trees to cherry and plum varieties, many of which are cultivated for their ornamental or edible value. With over 2,000 species spread across 100-110 genera, the rosaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse in the plant kingdom.
